description: User experience and interface designer responsible for creating intuitive, accessible, and visually appealing digital products. Bridges the gap between user needs, business goals, and technical feasibility through research, wireframing, prototyping, and visual design.

responsibilities: Create wireframes and low-fidelity mockups for new features
Assist in user research sessions and compile findings
Design UI components in Figma following design system guidelines
Prepare assets for developer handoff
Conduct basic usability testing and document feedback
Iterate on designs based on feedback from senior designers
Own end-to-end design for assigned features or products
Conduct user interviews and usability testing independently
Create and contribute to design system components
Develop user flows, journey maps, and information architecture
Present design rationale to stakeholders and defend decisions
Collaborate closely with developers during implementation
Mentor junior designers on process and tools
Lead design strategy for multiple product areas
Drive user research initiatives and translate insights into design decisions
Establish and evolve design systems and pattern libraries
Mentor design team members and conduct design reviews
Partner with product management on roadmap and feature definition
Advocate for user-centered design processes across the organization
Run design sprints and workshops with cross-functional teams

day_to_day: Morning standup with product team — align on priorities and blockers
Review design QA tickets — check implemented designs match specs pixel-perfect
Design sprint work — create wireframes, prototypes, or high-fidelity mockups for upcoming features
User research session — conduct interviews or usability tests with 3-5 participants
Design review with stakeholders — present work, gather feedback, iterate
Developer collaboration — sit with frontend team to review implementation, answer questions about design intent
Contribute to design system — create new components, update documentation, ensure consistency
Competitive analysis — review competitor products to identify UX gaps and opportunities
Self-learning — follow design blogs (Dribbble, Behance, UX Collective), experiment with new tools

discover: Stakeholder interviews to understand business goals
User interviews with target audience (6-8 participants)
Analytics review — identify drop-off points and user behavior patterns
define: Synthesize research findings into affinity maps
Map user journeys and identify pain points
Define problem statements and design hypotheses
prototype: Interactive prototypes for user testing (Prototype mode)
High-fidelity mockups with final visual design
test: Usability testing with 5 users per iteration
deliver: Design specification with measurements and states

common_issues.symptom: Developers implement designs incorrectly. Missing states, spacing off, colors don't match.
common_issues.root_cause: Design specs lack edge cases (loading, empty, error states). No annotation for interactions. Measurements not clearly defined.
common_issues.fix: Create comprehensive handoff specs with all component states. Include redlines for spacing. Annotate interactions with triggers, conditions, and feedback.
common_issues.prevention: Use design system components consistently. QA every implementation before release. Hold regular design-developer syncs.

common_issues.symptom: Developers push back during sprint planning saying design cannot be implemented within timeline.
common_issues.root_cause: Design created without understanding technical constraints. Complex animations. Custom components when library components would work.
common_issues.fix: Involve developers early in design process. Understand framework capabilities. Use existing component library when possible.
common_issues.prevention: Regular design-developer pairing sessions. Stay updated on frontend capabilities. Create feasibility checklist before finalizing designs.

common_issues.symptom: Navigation patterns differ between pages. Some use modals, some use full pages for similar actions.
common_issues.root_cause: No design system or pattern library. Multiple designers working without shared guidelines.
common_issues.fix: Audit existing UI for inconsistencies. Create unified design system with reusable components. Document interaction patterns.
common_issues.prevention: Maintain and evolve design system. Regular design reviews for consistency. Component-first design approach.

industry_standards: WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility compliance — color contrast, keyboard navigation, screen reader support
Material Design 3 / Human Interface Guidelines — platform-specific design conventions
Atomic Design methodology — atoms, molecules, organisms, templates, pages
8px grid system — consistent spacing and alignment using multiples of 8
Figma auto-layout for responsive design components
Design token architecture — colors, typography, spacing stored as centralized tokens
Usability testing with 5 users identifies 85% of usability issues (Nielsen Norman Group)
Design sprint framework (5-day process by Google Ventures)
Double Diamond design process — discover, define, develop, deliver

project_types.name: Mobile App Redesign
project_types.approach: Start with audit of existing UX issues, create user journey maps, design improved navigation, prototype key flows, test with users, iterate based on feedback

project_types.name: SaaS Dashboard
project_types.approach: Focus on information hierarchy, data visualization clarity, customizable layouts, dark mode support, accessibility for all data types

project_types.name: E-Commerce Experience
project_types.approach: Optimize conversion funnel, design clear product discovery, simplify checkout flow, mobile-first approach, trust signals and social proof
